Commerce Announces $50 Million Semiconductor Manufacturing Grant

May 20, 2024

The U.S. Department of Commerce today announced a $50 million grant to support domestic semiconductor manufacturing under the CHIPS and Science Act. The grant will be awarded to a leading American chipmaker to expand its fabrication facility in Arizona.

This investment is expected to create approximately 2,000 high-skilled manufacturing jobs and strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain. The facility will produce advanced logic chips used in defense, automotive, and AI applications.
